tools.name: check_outdoor_weather
tools.description: Check if the weather is suitable for outdoor activities for a specific location and time period. The function require location for the weather forecast. There are two options of parameters combination: 1) Latitude and Longitude coordinates (most precise), OR 2) A suburb and capital city name. If coordinates are provided, any suburb/capital input will be ignored. The user must also provide a start and end time for the activity in ISO format. The function will return two pieces of information: 1) A boolean indicating if the weather is suitable for outdoor activities, and 2) A brief summary of the weather conditions during the specified time period.

latitude.description: The latitude of the location, e.g., 37.7749

longitude.description: The longitude of the location, e.g., -122.4194

suburb.description: The suburb or city name, e.g., Parkside

capital.description: The capital city of the state, e.g., Adelaide

start_datetime.description: The start time of the outdoor activity in ISO format, e.g., 2023-10-01T14:00:00

end_datetime.description: The end time of the outdoor activity in ISO format, e.g., 2023-10-01T16:00:00

tools.name: convert_nl_to_iso_datetime
tools.description: Converts a natural language date and time string (e.g., 'tomorrow afternoon', 'next Friday at 3pm') to a machine-readable ISO 8601 formatted datetime string. The function assumes a default timezone of 'Australia/Adelaide' if not specified.

date_string.description: The natural language date and time to convert.

from_timezone.description: The timezone of the user's location (optional). Defaults to 'Australia/Adelaide'.
